headline: The Skeeters Just Went Full Thanos on North Correa
subheadline: A 9-0 shutout, the Twins keep quietly winning, and yes, we need to talk about the Buttville Skidmarks.
body:
Look, I've seen a lot of beatdowns in my day. I watched the '07 Patriots dismantle the league. I sat through every episode of Season 2 of The Bear stress-eating my way through Carmy's mental breakdown. But what the **Sugar Land Skeeters** just did to **North Correa**? That was violence. That was Goodfellas 'go get your shinebox' energy. A 9-0 shutout doesn't even feel real in fantasy baseball—it's like watching someone win a game of Monopoly by turn four.

(Side note: Is **North Correa** named after Carlos? Because if so, the irony of getting shut out while your namesake is probably making $30 million to hit .240 is *chef's kiss*.)

The matchup I actually want to discuss is **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** squeaking past **Hiroshima Radiation** 5.0-4.0. This is the kind of win that separates the real contenders from the pretenders—the Spurs over the Suns in 2007, the Patriots somehow beating the Rams in the first Super Bowl, Ted Lasso winning over the doubters one biscuit at a time. The Twins are doing something sneaky here. They're not flashy. They're not posting 9-0 wins. They're just... winning. Every. Single. Week. (Someone check if they have a 'Playoff Lenny' type on their roster who only shows up when it matters.)

Meanwhile, the **Canberra Wombats** handled business against the **Buttville Skidmarks** (a name I will never, ever get tired of typing) with a comfortable 7-3 victory. The Wombats are giving me 2011 Cardinals vibes—nobody's talking about them, but they're just lurking, waiting to ruin someone's October. The Skidmarks, meanwhile, continue to leave their mark on this league in exactly the way their name suggests.

(I'm legally required to mention that **Benchwarmers** beat **General Hospital** 6-4, which is exactly the kind of matchup name that makes me love beer league baseball. Somewhere, David Spade is warming up in the bullpen.)

Now here's where it gets weird: the standings show everyone at 0-0. Our commissioner is either asleep at the wheel, running an elaborate social experiment, or this is some Westworld-level simulation glitch. I'm going with option three until proven otherwise.

What's next? We need to see if the Skeeters can maintain this dominance or if North Correa becomes their villain origin story. And someone—ANYONE—needs to slow down the Twins before they quietly run away with this thing.

See you next week. Bring snacks.
